parameters.content:
## 🏛️ FinCEN Real Estate Reporter

### What this workflow does
1. Watches Google Drive folder for closing documents
2. Extracts buyer, seller, property, transaction data
3. Identifies beneficial owners for entity buyers
4. Checks if transaction meets reporting thresholds
5. Logs reportable transactions to Google Sheets
6. Sends Slack alert with FinCEN report data

### Setup steps
1. Get PDF Vector API key from pdfvector.com/api-keys
2. Create a Google Drive folder for closings
3. Create a Google Sheet with these columns:
   File # | Property | Transaction Type | Amount | Buyer Type | Buyer Name | Beneficial Owner | Seller | Reportable | Report Status | Processed Date
4. Update the folder ID and sheet ID in the nodes
5. Connect your Slack workspace

### Perfect for
- Title companies
- Real estate attorneys
- Closing agents

parameters.prompt: Extract real estate transaction data for FinCEN reporting. Return all fields flat — no nested objects. Extract: transactionType (sale/purchase/transfer), closingDate, fileNumber, propertyType (residential/commercial/mixed-use). Property address flat: propertyStreet, propertyCity, propertyState, propertyZip, propertyCounty. Transaction: purchasePrice (number), paymentMethod (cash/wire/check/financed), financingType (conventional/FHA/VA/none/cash). Buyer: buyerType (Individual/LLC/Corporation/Trust/Partnership), buyerLegalName, buyerAddress, buyerTinLast4, buyerFormationState, buyerFormationDate. Beneficial owners as plain text: beneficialOwnersSummary (name and ownership % comma separated), beneficialOwnersCount (number). Seller: sellerType, sellerLegalName, sellerAddress. Parties: titleCompany, closingAgent, additionalParties (plain text list of roles and names).
